classification: AI data preparation and corpus operations
header_tagline: Fast Corpus for European AI teams
summary: Clean PDF and text backlogs to AI-ready JSONL with extracted text, chunks, embeddings, stable IDs, and an inspectable run report at a predictable starting unit price. Adjacent scoped use cases include recurring public-data quality runs over public or test metadata exports.
positioning: Customers buy completed corpus output and run evidence, not GPU-hours, API/CLI access, an orchestration layer, or a platform project.
build_vs_buy_positioning: A team can build and operate its own extraction, chunking, embedding, queueing, retry, and reporting pipeline, or buy a completed Fast Corpus run. Solvyr customers do not provision workers, operate queues, or assemble the pipeline.
cost_positioning: Cost-effective backlog processing with a scoped run, price per processed page, and a decision based on inspected output.
api_cli_positioning: API/CLI language is integration or handoff context, not the primary public offer.
european_sovereignty_positioning: European execution control and sovereignty are relevant for archives, public-sector teams, research networks, and EU/NL connectors when tied to a real document backlog and pilot decision.
broader_system: Controlled distributed execution for async AI workloads with central scheduling, bounded tasks, retries, visible outcomes, and completed work rather than raw capacity rental.
best_for: public-data metadata quality checks before publication, harvesting, indexing, or downstream reuse
teams that want a cost-effective corpus run without building another internal pipeline
archives, libraries, municipalities, research networks, and public-sector teams that want European execution-control context
public or semi-public connector introductions tied to real document-heavy AI workloads
not_for: custom structured extraction in the current trial
highly sensitive data without prior agreement
supported_inputs: public or test catalog metadata exports scoped as public-data quality runs
outputs: issue CSV/JSON, record-level notes, suggested fixes, and caveats for public-data quality runs

starting_price.taxes: excluding VAT and taxes unless agreed otherwise
starting_price.qualification: standard clean digital PDF/text batch processing

security_boundary: Low- or moderate-sensitivity pilot workloads. Sensitive or regulated data requires prior agreement. For archive, public-sector, and European AI pilots, execution location, storage location, retention, deletion, and operator access can be made explicit before data is shared; this is governance context, not a blanket compliance or confidentiality guarantee.

published_proof.scope: Measured internal clean digital PDF run
published_proof.supports: Clean digital PDF/text extraction, chunking, and embedding generation

direct_answers.what_is_solvyr: A managed B2B service that turns bounded document and text backlogs into accepted AI-ready corpus output with run evidence.
direct_answers.is_solvyr_a_gpu_provider: No. Customers buy completed corpus work; controlled distributed execution is supporting infrastructure.
direct_answers.is_solvyr_an_orchestration_layer: No. Orchestration runs underneath the managed corpus service; customers buy the completed run rather than operating the scheduler or pipeline.
direct_answers.what_fast_corpus_delivers: Extracted text, AI-ready JSONL, chunks, embeddings, stable IDs, caveats, and an inspectable run report.
direct_answers.how_to_start: Submit a bounded backlog and output target, then use a representative non-sensitive sample for a capped pilot run.
